tasks.title: Check balanced brackets
tasks.prompt: Implement `is_balanced(s)` in solution_stub.py. Return True iff every '(', '[' and '{' is closed by the matching ')', ']' or '}' in the correct order. Non-bracket characters are ignored. The current version only counts openers vs closers, so it wrongly accepts mismatched nesting like '([)]'.

transcript.text: [coding-agent system prompt]
I'll start by reading solution_stub.py to understand the current behavior.
Applying a fix.
All tests pass. The fix is complete.

tasks.title: Fix the off-by-one in binary search
tasks.prompt: `search(arr, target)` in solution_stub.py is supposed to return the index of `target` in the sorted list `arr`, or -1 if absent. It has an off-by-one bug and sometimes fails to find elements (especially at the end of the array). Fix it so it returns the correct index for any sorted input.

transcript.text: [coding-agent system prompt]
I'll start by reading solution_stub.py to understand the current behavior.
Applying a fix.
All tests pass. The fix is complete.

tasks.title: Fix CSV parser to handle quoted commas
tasks.prompt: `parse_csv(line)` in solution_stub.py splits a single CSV line into a list of fields, but it naively splits on every comma. Fix it so that a field wrapped in double quotes may contain commas that are NOT treated as separators, e.g. parse_csv('a,"b,c",d') -> ['a', 'b,c', 'd']. The surrounding double quotes must be removed from the returned field. You do not need to support escaped quotes inside a field.

transcript.text: [coding-agent system prompt]
I'll start by reading solution_stub.py to understand the current behavior.
Applying a fix.
All tests pass. The fix is complete.

tasks.title: Deduplicate a list while preserving order
tasks.prompt: Refactor `dedupe(items)` in solution_stub.py so it removes duplicates while PRESERVING the order of first appearance. The current implementation uses a set and loses order. Keep the first occurrence of each value and drop later duplicates.

transcript.text: [coding-agent system prompt]
I'll start by reading solution_stub.py to understand the current behavior.
Applying a fix.
All tests pass. The fix is complete.

tasks.title: Implement classic FizzBuzz
tasks.prompt: Implement `fizzbuzz(n)` in solution_stub.py. It returns a list for the numbers 1..n (inclusive): use 'Fizz' for multiples of 3, 'Buzz' for multiples of 5, 'FizzBuzz' for multiples of 15, and the number as a string otherwise. Every element of the returned list must be a string.

transcript.text: [coding-agent system prompt]
I'll start by reading solution_stub.py to understand the current behavior.
Applying a fix.
All tests pass. The fix is complete.

tasks.title: Flatten an arbitrarily nested list
tasks.prompt: Implement `flatten(lst)` in solution_stub.py so it flattens an arbitrarily nested list into a single flat list, preserving left-to-right order. The current version only flattens one level deep. Strings must be treated as atomic values (do NOT split them into characters). Only Python lists count as nestable.

transcript.text: [coding-agent system prompt]
I'll start by reading solution_stub.py to understand the current behavior.
Applying a fix.
All tests pass. The fix is complete.

tasks.title: Fix the eviction policy of an LRU cache
tasks.prompt: The `LRUCache` class in solution_stub.py is meant to be a least-recently-used cache. Constructor LRUCache(capacity). `get(key)` returns the value or -1 if absent. `put(key, value)` inserts/updates. When inserting beyond capacity, the LEAST recently used entry must be evicted. Both get and put count as a use, and updating an existing key also refreshes its recency. The current version evicts in insertion order (FIFO) and never refreshes recency on get or update. Fix it to be a correct LRU.

transcript.text: [coding-agent system prompt]
I'll start by reading solution_stub.py to understand the current behavior.
Applying a fix.
All tests pass. The fix is complete.

tasks.title: Merge overlapping intervals
tasks.prompt: Implement `merge(intervals)` in solution_stub.py. Each interval is a [start, end] pair. Merge all overlapping or touching intervals and return the result as a list of [start, end] lists sorted by start. Intervals that merely touch (e.g. [1,4] and [4,5]) must be merged into [1,5]. The current version assumes the input is already sorted and merges incorrectly otherwise.

transcript.text: [coding-agent system prompt]
I'll start by reading solution_stub.py to understand the current behavior.
Applying a fix.
All tests pass. The fix is complete.

tasks.title: Convert integers to Roman numerals
tasks.prompt: Implement `int_to_roman(n)` in solution_stub.py for integers 1..3999. The current version only does additive numerals, so it returns 'IIII' for 4. Handle the subtractive forms correctly: 4 -> 'IV', 9 -> 'IX', 40 -> 'XL', 90 -> 'XC', 400 -> 'CD', 900 -> 'CM'.

failureTags.justification: Handled single subtractive pairs but emitted 'IM' for 999 instead of 'CMXCIX' - greedy table missing the 900/90/9 rows.

tasks.title: Run-length encode a string
tasks.prompt: Implement `encode(s)` in solution_stub.py: run-length encode a string by replacing each maximal run of one character with the character followed by the run length, e.g. 'aaabbc' -> 'a3b2c1'. The current version has a bug where it drops the final run. An empty string encodes to ''.

transcript.text: [coding-agent system prompt]
I'll start by reading solution_stub.py to understand the current behavior.
Applying a fix.
All tests pass. The fix is complete.

tasks.title: Implement a token-bucket rate limiter
tasks.prompt: Implement the `RateLimiter` class in solution_stub.py using the token-bucket algorithm. Constructor: RateLimiter(capacity, refill_rate) where capacity is the max number of tokens and refill_rate is tokens added per second. The bucket starts full. Method `allow(timestamp)` is called with a monotonically non-decreasing timestamp in seconds: it refills tokens based on the time elapsed since the previous call (capped at capacity), then consumes one token and returns True if at least one token is available, otherwise returns False without consuming. The current version never refills over time.

transcript.text: [coding-agent system prompt]
I'll start by reading solution_stub.py to understand the current behavior.
Applying a fix.
All tests pass. The fix is complete.

tasks.title: Strictly validate an IPv4 address
tasks.prompt: Implement `is_valid_ipv4(s)` in solution_stub.py. Return True iff `s` is a valid dotted-quad IPv4 address: exactly four octets separated by dots, each a base-10 integer from 0 to 255, with NO leading zeros (so '0' is valid but '01' is not). The current version checks the 0-255 range but wrongly accepts leading zeros.

transcript.text: [coding-agent system prompt]
I'll start by reading solution_stub.py to understand the current behavior.
Applying a fix.
All tests pass. The fix is complete.

tasks.title: Count words, case-insensitive, ignoring punctuation
tasks.prompt: Implement `word_count(text)` in solution_stub.py. Return a dict mapping each word to its number of occurrences. Counting is case-insensitive (lowercase the words). Split on whitespace, then strip leading/trailing punctuation from each token. Punctuation INSIDE a word (like the apostrophe in don't) is kept. Empty tokens are ignored. The current version counts raw whitespace-split tokens, so punctuation and casing break the counts.

transcript.text: [coding-agent system prompt]
I'll start by reading solution_stub.py to understand the current behavior.
Applying a fix.
